Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a shopping cart for a user, containing a list of prompts they intend to purchase.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this cart. (Relationship: User 1:1 Cart)

promptIds.description: References to Prompts in the cart. (Relationship: Cart 1:N Prompt)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the cart was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the cart was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a completed order placed by a user.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

promptIds.description: References to Prompts included in the order. (Relationship: Order 1:N Prompt)

orderDate.description: Date and time when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: Total amount paid for the order.

paymentMethod.description: Payment method used for the order (e.g., credit card, PayPal).

status.description: Status of the order (e.g., pending, processing, shipped, delivered).

definition.description: Stores shopping carts for each user. Path-based ownership ensures clear access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the cart.

definition.description: Stores order data for each user. Path-based ownership ensures that users can only access their own order history.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who placed the order.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to support a shopping cart and order management system for the PromptVerse application. It focuses on user-centric data organization and authorization independence.  The `/users/{userId}/carts` collection stores individual user carts, linking each cart directly to a user. This path-based ownership ensures clear ownership and straightforward security rules. The `/users/{userId}/orders` collection similarly stores user-specific order data. Both cart and order data are segregated into user-specific subcollections to maintain a homogeneous security posture. This design avoids complex queries and simplifies security rules, as access control is primarily based on the authenticated user's ID. The absence of a global 'carts' or 'orders' collection enforces that users can only access their own carts and orders. This structure facilitates secure `list` operations because each user's data is isolated within their own subcollection. No denormalization is explicitly required in this basic cart/order structure as ownership is directly implied by the path.
